title: Retrofit
objectives: Generate a typed HTTP client from annotations

sections.markdown: `retrofit` (Dart) is a code-generated, typed HTTP client on top of Dio, inspired by Square's Retrofit for Android. You declare an abstract class with `@GET`/`@POST` etc., and the generator writes the implementation.

Retrofit's win is **typed, discoverable, testable** APIs: the client is an interface — you swap in a fake for tests. Combined with json_serializable + freezed models, you get near-zero boilerplate networking with compile-time safety.
Forgetting to re-run build_runner after annotation/model changes — 'method not found' errors on generated symbols. Add `dart run build_runner watch` in your dev loop.

Pass a configured Dio (with your interceptors) into the Retrofit factory. Keep endpoint URLs relative to baseUrl. For OpenAPI schemas, consider `openapi_generator` — Retrofit still shines for small/handwritten schemas.

flashcards.back: The implementation of your abstract API class from `@GET/@POST/…` annotations, on top of Dio.
Your code depends on the abstract interface; fakes are trivial to inject.

quiz.q: Symbols not found after adding an @GET method?
quiz.explain: Codegen must re-run to update generated files.

challenges.text: Swap the real Dio for a fake in a widget test.
Generate a large API from OpenAPI; keep the interface stable across versions.

interviewQuestions: How does Retrofit generate its client?
Retrofit + Dio + json_serializable — who does what?
How do you test a Retrofit-based repository?
